headlines.title: Stealing from the gods: India’s Ram Temple hit by corruption scandal
headlines.snippet: India's Ram Temple is facing a corruption scandal with embezzlement allegations. This is embarrassing for PM Modi's government, especially ahead of crucial state elections.

headlines.title: Skydiving plane crash kills 11 in northeastern France
headlines.snippet: A plane belonging to a parachuting school crashed in Tomblaine, France, resulting in the deaths of 11 people.

headlines.title: Thousands of Kurds gather in Turkiye to demand release of PKK leader
headlines.snippet: Thousands of Kurds gathered in Turkiye to demand the release of PKK leader Abdullah Öcalan and other prisoners.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
headlines.snippet: Oil prices have increased as strikes involving the US and Iran threaten the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway.

headlines.title: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase
headlines.snippet: An Australian man, Simon Peter Carman, has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla in a suitcase.

headlines.title: Saudi Crown Prince and Macron discuss US-Iran agreement, peace efforts
headlines.snippet: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and French Prime Minister Emmanuel Macron discussed a Memorandum of Understanding between the US and Iran, along with regional developments. They also emphasized the importance of freedom of navigation and diplomatic efforts to reduce tensions.

headlines.title: Morning update
headlines.snippet: Reports suggest the United States and Iran are returning to diplomacy, with an agreement to halt military strikes and resume talks in Doha. Communication channels remain active despite unresolved tensions over the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Iranian minister discusses Hormuz management with Omani counterpart
headlines.snippet: Iran's deputy foreign minister, Kazem Gharibabadi, discussed the future management of the Strait of Hormuz with Oman's Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, Abdulaziz Al-Hinai. The exchange occurred within the framework of an MoU and coastal states' sovereign rights.

headlines.title: Israeli cyber chief reports sharp rise in Iranian cyberattacks after war
headlines.snippet: A senior Israeli security official stated that Iranian cyberattacks against Israel have sharply increased since the US-Israeli offensive. The number of incidents rose significantly from approximately 1,600 in June 2025 to about 4,800 in June 2026.

headlines.title: In photos: Reshadat; Persian Gulf’s largest oil processing platform
